source.title: (38) How I Built My Own AI Trading Assistant Using Claude Cowork - YouTube

rawText:
[YouTube Description]
176K views  2 weeks ago


  
  176,027 views • Mar 24, 2026




          
          
          
          In this video, I’m showing you how to turn Claude Cowork into your ultimate 24/7 trading assistant using Anthropic's brand-new Computer Use and Dispatch features. You’ll learn how to set up an AI agent that doesn't just talk, but actually controls your desktop, analyzes your local trade journals, and executes live positions on Blofin via API or automated TradingView webhooks. …...more
...more

plan.summary: This YouTube video demonstrates how to build an AI trading assistant using Claude Cowork and Anthropic's Computer Use/Dispatch features, with practical integration for desktop control, trade analysis, and automated trading. The only available actionable content is to watch the video for full instructions.

steps.rationale: Watching the video provides step-by-step instructions on setting up and using Claude Cowork as an AI trading assistant.

rawText: 1. CodeEx Plugin — Uses OpenAI Codex CLI for adversarial code review [01:22]. 2. Obsidian & Obsidian Skills — Searchable local knowledge base, vault of subfolders + markdown = mini RAG [03:51]. 3. Auto Research — Automated ML experimenter, commits improvements, discards regressions [05:23]. 4. AwesomeDesign.md — Frontend design templates based on popular sites (Notion, Figma) [06:25]. 5. Firecrawl CLI & Skill — Advanced web scraping, bypasses anti-bot, LLM-optimized data [08:15]. 6. Playwright CLI — Browser automation via accessibility tree [10:21]. 7. NotebookLM-Pine — Connects Claude to Google NotebookLM for heavy doc analysis [11:35]. 8. Skill Creator Skill — Build and test custom skills with benchmarking [12:42]. 9. LightRAG — Lightweight GraphRAG for massive document sets [13:30]. 10. GWS CLI — Google Workspace integration with workflow recipes [15:02].

plan.summary: Install 10 recommended Claude Code plugins covering code review, knowledge management, design, web scraping, browser automation, document analysis, skill creation, RAG, and Google Workspace integration.

params.text: Install obsidian-pkm@3.6.2 as MCP server with vault at ~/.openclaw/vault/

steps.rationale: Set up Obsidian PKM vault with 20 MCP tools for knowledge management
Add headless browser automation via accessibility tree
Connect to Google NotebookLM for document analysis offloading

params.text: Register Firecrawl MCP: npx -y firecrawl-mcp (needs API key)

steps.rationale: Add web scraping with anti-bot bypass
Connect Notion workspace using existing API token
OpenAI Codex CLI for adversarial code review (already had skill)
Firecrawl CLI companion to MCP server
Google Workspace CLI with 100+ skills and workflow recipes
66 brand DESIGN.md templates for frontend work
Skill creation tool with benchmarking and A/B testing

params.text: Skipped LightRAG — obsidian-pkm covers RAG use case

steps.rationale: LightRAG requires Docker + separate LLM backend; overkill for current scale

stepResults.output: obsidian-pkm MCP connected, vault created at ~/.openclaw/vault/
NotebookLM MCP connected (needs first-time browser auth)
Notion MCP connected using existing ntn_ token
gws 0.22.5 installed at /opt/homebrew/bin/gws (needs gws auth setup)
